summary: Ukraine and Russia conducted a prisoner exchange of 185 personnel each, with 185 Ukrainian defenders returning home from Russian captivity, some held since 2022. Russia launched a large-scale drone attack overnight on June 5, deploying 216 Shahed-type and other drones plus two Kh-59/69 guided missiles, with Ukrainian air defenses claiming 198 drones shot down or suppressed. Ukrainian drone forces struck vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports and took fire control of Donetsk Airport territory, while a sea drone explosion occurred at Romania's Constanta port.
key_points: Ukraine and Russia exchanged 185 prisoners each; 185 Ukrainian defenders returned from Russian captivity, some held since 2022. (Source: @UkraineNow/81705, @tass_agency/379306)
Russia launched 216 Shahed-type drones and two Kh-59/69 missiles overnight June 5; Ukraine claims 198 drones shot down/suppressed, 16 hits recorded at 13 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)
Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces struck five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports, targeting ships illegally transporting grain, fuel, and military cargo. (Source: @UkraineNow/81704)
Ukrainian drone operators took fire control of Donetsk Airport territory, destroying Russian launch vehicles, cars, and crews on the runway. (Source: @UkraineNow/81679)
A sea drone exploded at Romania's Constanta port; Romanian PM and President Nicușor Dan were notified, no casualties reported. (Source: @UkraineNow/81696, @eRadarrua/95498)
Drones struck two cargo ships in the Azov Sea, killing 5 Azerbaijani sailors and injuring 3; Ukrainian SBS commander Brovdi confirmed the attack. (Source: @tass_agency/379274, @ukr_leaks_eng/30703)
Zelensky published an open letter to Putin proposing negotiations and a personal meeting in a third country, with Ukraine offering a full ceasefire during talks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30689)

Russia's Southern Group of Forces artillery struck up to 15 Ukrainian soldiers; Russian MoD claims two settlements liberated in the SVO zone over the past week. (Source: @tass_agency/379291, @tass_agency/379270)

sections.executive_summary: The most operationally significant development is the large-scale Russian drone attack on the night of June 5, involving 216 Shahed-type drones and two Kh-59/69 guided missiles launched from multiple directions including Orel, Kursk, Bryansk, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Chauda in occupied Crimea. Ukrainian air defenses claimed to have shot down or suppressed 198 drones, though 16 strikes were recorded at 13 locations with debris falling at 12 additional sites. Simultaneously, Ukrainian forces conducted significant offensive drone operations, striking vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports and establishing fire control over Donetsk Airport territory.

points: Ukrainian air defenses intercepted 198 out of 216 enemy drones during the overnight attack of June 5, with 16 Shahed-type drones hitting 13 locations and debris falling at 12 additional sites across northern, southern, and eastern Ukraine. The attack also included two Kh-59/69 guided missiles from occupied Zaporizhzhia region which failed to reach their targets. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)

Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces under commander Brovdi (Magyar) struck five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports, targeting cargo ships and tankers illegally transporting grain, fuel, and military cargo from occupied territories. (Source: @UkraineNow/81704)

Ukrainian drone operators took fire control of Donetsk Airport territory, the first such operation in modern Ukrainian military history, destroying Russian launch vehicles, trucks, and crews directly on the runway to prevent its use as a Shahed base. (Source: @UkraineNow/81679)

Ukrainian drones struck two cargo ships 'Natra' and 'Zircon' in the Azov Sea, killing 5 Azerbaijani sailors and injuring 3; the Ukrainian SBS commander confirmed responsibility for the attack. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30703, @eRadarrua/95521)

A sea drone exploded at Romania's Constanta port, prompting notification of Romanian PM and President Nicușor Dan; no casualties were reported, and Romania's Ministry of Interior announced a press conference. (Source: @UkraineNow/81696, @eRadarrua/95498)

Zelensky published an open letter to Putin proposing negotiations and a personal meeting in a third country, offering a complete ceasefire during negotiations with US oversight. Russia's Lavrov stated no letter was received through diplomatic channels. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30689, @tass_agency/379305)

Russia attacked Brovary in Kyiv Oblast with drones, killing 4 people and injuring 4 others at a civilian food industry enterprise; Zaporizhzhia was also hit with 10 people injured in a residential high-rise. (Source: @UkraineNow/81692, @UkraineNow/81681)

Russian forces launched a massive overnight drone attack on June 5 deploying 216 Shahed-type attack drones (including jet-powered variants), Gerbera, Italmas, Banderol loitering munitions, and Parody-type decoy drones from Orel, Kursk, Bryansk, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Chauda in occupied Crimea, plus two Kh-59/69 guided missiles. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)

Russia's Ministry of Defense reported that Russian forces liberated two settlements in the SVO zone over the past week, and the Southern Group of Forces artillery struck up to 15 Ukrainian soldiers. (Source: @tass_agency/379270, @tass_agency/379291)
Russia conducted a prisoner exchange with Ukraine on a 185-for-185 basis, with the Russian MoD confirming the swap. (Source: @tass_agency/379306)
The Russian Ministry of Defense reported the liberation of the village of Gulyaipolskoye (Komsomolskoye) in Zaporozhye region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678)
Two Tu-95MS strategic bombers were relocated from Olenya airbase to Ukrainka airbase in the Russian Far East. (Source: @eRadarrua/95534)

Putin stated that Russia's constitution allows him to run for president in 2030 but said it is 'too early to discuss'; he also received briefings on world leaders' reactions to Zelensky's letter. (Source: @tass_agency/379294, @tass_agency/379273)

Lavrov stated Russia received no letter from Zelensky through diplomatic channels; Peskov said the Zelensky letter topic would come up at the SPIEF plenary session and that no Putin-Trump meeting is planned yet. (Source: @tass_agency/379305, @tass_agency/379272, @tass_agency/379269)

Ukrainian drone operators took fire control of Donetsk Airport territory, destroying Russian Shahed launch vehicles, transport trucks, and enemy crews directly on the runway, preventing use of the airfield as a drone base. (Source: @UkraineNow/81679)

Russian Shahed drones were tracked heading toward Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, and Pavlohrad from multiple directions, with jet-powered Shahed variants detected heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south. (Source: @kpszsu/64094, @kpszsu/64096, @mon1tor_ua/67488)
Russian forces liberated the village of Gulyaipolskoye (Komsomolskoye) in Zaporozhye region according to the Russian MoD. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678)
Russian artillery from the Southern Group of Forces struck Ukrainian positions, reportedly hitting up to 15 Ukrainian soldiers. (Source: @tass_agency/379291)

Shahed drones were tracked over Dnipropetrovsk Oblast heading toward Apostolove and Kryvyi Rih, with additional drones detected over northern Kharkiv Oblast heading southward toward Chuhuiv and Kharkiv. (Source: @kpszsu/64112, @kpszsu/64115, @eRadarrua/95517)
The Russian MoD reported liberation of two settlements in the SVO zone over the past week, though specific names beyond Gulyaipolskoye were not detailed in the messages. (Source: @tass_agency/379270)

Multiple Shahed drone groups were tracked over Sumy Oblast heading toward Bahmach, Putyvl, and Novhorod-Siverskyi, with additional drones detected near Hlukhiv and Svesa moving northward. (Source: @kpszsu/64121, @kpszsu/64091, @kpszsu/64115, @eRadarrua/95516)

Drones were tracked over Chernihiv Oblast heading toward Ichnia, Bakhmach, Baturyn, Borzna, Snovsk, Korukyvka, and Novhorod-Siverskyi, with one drone detected over Chornobyl Exclusion Zone heading toward Zhytomyr Oblast. (Source: @eRadarrua/95482, @eRadarrua/95480, @kpszsu/64085)

A high-speed target was detected over Kyiv Oblast heading westward from the east; Brovary in Kyiv Oblast was struck by drones killing 4 and injuring 4 at a food industry enterprise. (Source: @kpszsu/64119, @UkraineNow/81692)
Three Shahed drones were tracked over Zhytomyr Oblast heading toward Rivne Oblast/Korosten, with one identified as a relay drone near Ovruch. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67492, @eRadarrua/95477)
Ballistic weapon threats were issued from the south and southeast, indicating potential missile strikes on northern regions. (Source: @kpszsu/64116, @kpszsu/64105)

Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces struck five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports, targeting cargo ships and tankers used for illegal transport of grain, fuel, and military cargo from occupied territories. (Source: @UkraineNow/81704)

Ukrainian drones struck cargo ships 'Natra' and 'Zircon' in the Azov Sea, killing 5 Azerbaijani sailors and injuring 3; the attack was confirmed by Ukrainian SBS commander Brovdi. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30703, @eRadarrua/95521)

A sea drone exploded at Romania's Constanta port, with local media reporting a drone blast; no casualties were reported and Romanian leadership was immediately notified. (Source: @UkraineNow/81696, @eRadarrua/95498)

Shahed drones were launched from Shatalovo heading toward Ukraine, with additional drones detected over the Black Sea heading toward Bilhorod-Dnistrovskyi district of Odesa Oblast, Chornomorsk, and Serhiivka. (Source: @eRadarrua/95488, @kpszsu/64113, @mon1tor_ua/67490)
Russian forces used Kh-59/69 guided missiles launched from temporarily occupied Zaporizhzhia Oblast territory; both missiles failed to reach their targets. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)
Zaporizhzhia city was attacked with 10 people injured and a residential high-rise damaged in a sleeping district. (Source: @UkraineNow/81681)

Russia launched 216 Shahed-type attack drones (including jet-powered variants), Gerbera, Italmas, Banderol loitering munitions, and Parody decoy drones from Orel, Kursk, Bryansk, Primorsko-Akhtarsk, and Chauda in occupied Crimea during the overnight attack of June 5. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)

Ukrainian air defenses including aviation, anti-aircraft missile troops, EW units, drone units, and mobile fire groups shot down or suppressed 198 drones; 16 Shahed drones hit 13 locations with debris at 12 additional sites. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)
Two Tu-95MS strategic bombers were relocated from Olenya airbase to Ukrainka airbase in the Russian Far East, potentially indicating force preservation or repositioning. (Source: @eRadarrua/95534)
Ballistic weapon threats were issued twice from southern and southeastern directions, suggesting potential Iskander or similar missile launches. (Source: @kpszsu/64116, @kpszsu/64105)

A jet-powered Shahed variant was detected heading toward Zaporizhzhia from the south, and multiple drone groups were tracked across Sumy, Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, and Odesa oblasts. (Source: @kpszsu/64094, @kpszsu/64085)
The Kh-59/69 guided missiles launched from occupied Zaporizhzhia Oblast failed to reach their intended targets. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)

key_developments: Russia launched a massive overnight drone attack on June 5 with 216 Shahed-type drones and two Kh-59/69 guided missiles from multiple directions; Ukraine claims 198 drones intercepted, 16 hits at 13 locations. (Source: @kpszsu/64087)

Ukraine and Russia conducted a 185-for-185 prisoner exchange, with 185 Ukrainian defenders returning from Russian captivity including those held since 2022 from Mariupol, Azovstal, and multiple front sectors. (Source: @UkraineNow/81705, @tass_agency/379306)

Ukrainian Unmanned Systems Forces struck five vessels in Mariupol and Berdyanetsk ports, targeting ships illegally transporting grain, fuel, and military cargo from occupied territories. (Source: @UkraineNow/81704)
Ukrainian drone operators took fire control of Donetsk Airport territory for the first time, destroying Russian Shahed launch vehicles, trucks, and crews on the runway. (Source: @UkraineNow/81679)

Ukrainian drones struck cargo ships 'Natra' and 'Zircon' in the Azov Sea killing 5 Azerbaijani sailors; a sea drone also exploded at Romania's Constanta port with no casualties. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30703, @UkraineNow/81696)

Zelensky published an open letter to Putin proposing negotiations and a personal meeting in a third country with a full ceasefire offer; Russia denied receiving it through diplomatic channels. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30689, @tass_agency/379305)

Russian MoD reported liberating Gulyaipolskoye (Komsomolskoye) in Zaporozhye region and two settlements total in the SVO zone over the past week; Southern Group artillery struck up to 15 Ukrainian soldiers. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30678, @tass_agency/379270, @tass_agency/379291)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo is escalating significantly, with Russia demonstrating capacity for large-scale drone salvos of 216+ Shahed-type drones combined with guided missiles, targeting northern (Sumy, Chernihiv, Kyiv oblasts), eastern (Dnipro, Zaporizhzhia, Kharkiv), and southern (Odesa) regions simultaneously. Ukraine's air defense remains largely effective with a claimed 91.7% interception rate, but 16 successful strikes indicate saturation tactics are partially working. Ukrainian offensive drone operations are expanding in scope, now targeting Russian logistics vessels in Azov Sea ports and achieving tactical firsts like fire control of Donetsk Airport. The Azov Sea attacks on foreign-flagged vessels carrying Azerbaijani crews risk diplomatic complications. The Constanta port drone explosion in NATO-member Romania represents a potential escalation risk to alliance territory.
sections.regional_response: Romania responded to the sea drone explosion at Constanta port by immediately notifying the Prime Minister and President Nicușor Dan, with the Ministry of Interior scheduling a press conference. Azerbaijan confirmed 5 citizens killed and 3 injured in Ukrainian drone strikes on its ships in the Azov Sea. France's Macron expressed European interest in building dialogue with Russia on security and good neighborliness. The US supported a Ukraine aid bill with Rubio announcing an additional $400 million package, while stating relations with Russia would improve after the war ends. Iran confirmed a special passage regime for Russian vessels through the Strait of Hormuz.
sections.intelligence_notes: The relocation of two Tu-95MS bombers from Olenya to Ukrainka in the Russian Far East may indicate force preservation concerns or strategic repositioning away from Ukrainian long-range strike range. Russia's use of Parody-type decoy drones alongside real Shaheds, Gerbera, Italmas, and Banderol munitions shows evolving saturation tactics designed to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses. The Ukrainian claim of taking fire control of Donetsk Airport via drone operations represents a novel tactical approach that, if sustained, could degrade Russian Shahed launch capabilities in the eastern sector. The GUR assessment that Russia can deploy up to 100 ballistic missiles per month suggests continued high-intensity missile threats. The SPIEF forum is being used as a platform for Russian messaging on the conflict, with Putin's participation and discussion of Zelensky's letter.
